@graph.name: Mercedes-AMG models fitted with the M159/M178 family

mainEntity.name: How much does an M159/M178 engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned M159/M178 typically costs £10,000–£18,000 supply only [ESTIMATED], varying by variant and whether ancillaries are included. Fitting adds £1,100–£2,300 depending on labour rates, with specialist AMG workshops essential.

mainEntity.name: Is the M159/M178 a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M159/M178 rate 4.5★ out of 5 based on specialist feedback [MB-INTERVIEW]. They are robust at their core, but their high performance and exotic nature mean maintenance costs are substantial.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common M159/M178 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Oil leaks (valve cover gaskets and cam covers) are most common. M178 turbocharger failures and coolant leaks are also reported, especially on tracked or tuned examples.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes models use the M159/M178?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M159 powers the SLS AMG, and the M178 powers the AMG GT (Coupe, Roadster, Black Series) and the AMG GT 4-Door (M177 variant) — flagship sports cars and supercars.

mainEntity.name: How long does the M159/M178 last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With meticulous maintenance and limited track abuse, the M159/M178 can exceed 100,000 miles. However, many are low-mileage garage queens — and high-mileage examples are rare and require specialist inspection.

hero.tagPill: Mercedes-Benz • M159/M178 GT • Petrol • V8 6.2L NA / 4.0L TT • 2010–present
hero.h1: Mercedes-Benz M159/M178 GT Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The Mercedes M159/M178 are AMG's ultimate V8s — hand-built, sonorous, and immensely powerful, but expensive to maintain and repair. Backed by UK specialist insight [MB-INTERVIEW].

verdictRating.confidence: 85% confidence — based on specialist feedback and enthusiast data; these are lower-volume, high-performance engines.

rows.score: 12 (Some manageable repairs (oil leaks), but many failures are all-or-nothing events)
12 (Available but with wait times; AMG-specific parts can be dealer-only or import-dependent)
20 (Reconditioned cost is under 30% of high-value host vehicles (£40k+))

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: The M159/M178 are sensational, sonorous V8s — but they're AMG exotica: expensive to maintain and repair — buy with a warranty and a healthy budget for running costs.
verdictRating.bestFor: Enthusiasts wanting a flagship AMG V8 experience.
verdictRating.avoidIf: You're on a tight budget for maintenance and repairs.

rows.variantBadge: GT, GT S, GT C, GT R
GT Black Series, GT Track Series

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an M159/M178 typically takes 16–24 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [ESTIMATED].
